risks: Public network access enabled on ACR, Cosmos DB, and Key Vault — lacks network isolation recommended for production e-commerce workloads
Redis Standard tier does not support zone redundancy or VNet integration, creating a single-zone failure point for caching layer
Service Bus Standard tier lacks zone redundancy and dedicated throughput capacity, risking message processing delays under load
No RBAC role assignment resources defined (acrPull for managed identity on ACR, Key Vault Secrets User for managed identity on Key Vault)
No resource tagging strategy for cost allocation and operational governance across the 10 resources
correctionsRecommended: Add private endpoints for Cosmos DB, ACR, and Key Vault to restrict public network access and improve security posture
Upgrade Redis to Premium tier for zone redundancy, VNet injection, and data persistence in production
Consider upgrading Service Bus to Premium tier for zone redundancy, dedicated capacity, and VNet integration
Add explicit Microsoft.Authorization/roleAssignments resources for acrPull (identity → ACR) and Key Vault Secrets User (identity → Key Vault)
Define a resource tagging convention (environment, team, cost center) and apply across all resources
